sourceBlocks.title: How do I export my video as a SCORM package?
How can I restrict access to videos using SSO?

capabilityFacts.value: Synthesia can generate videos from prompts, URLs, documents, or scripts through the AI video assistant.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The video creation guide lists prompt, URL, document, and script as assistant inputs before generating an outline and then a video.

capabilityFacts.value: Synthesia provides stock AI avatars, personal avatars, and branded on-brand avatars depending on plan.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The pricing page breaks out AI avatars, personal avatars, and custom on-brand avatars by plan.

capabilityFacts.value: Synthesia supports dubbing, one-click translations, captions, and a multilingual player.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The localization feature page explicitly groups AI dubbing, video translator, multilingual player, and auto-captions into one flow.

capabilityFacts.value: Templates are a first-class creation path in both the editor and the API.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The video creation guide and create-from-template API reference both treat templates as a primary video creation flow.

capabilityFacts.value: Free and Starter are capped at 10 minutes per month, Creator at 30 minutes, while Enterprise is listed as unlimited.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The pricing page explicitly maps monthly video minutes to each plan.

capabilityFacts.value: Downloading videos and removing the Synthesia logo require at least Starter.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The pricing page lists download your videos and remove Synthesia logo under Starter and above.

capabilityFacts.value: A single video can contain up to 150 scenes, each up to 5 minutes, with a total duration cap of 4 hours.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The video creation guide states all three limits directly.

capabilityFacts.value: Unused monthly video minutes do not roll over into the next billing period.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The pricing FAQ explicitly says unused minutes do not roll over.

capabilityFacts.value: Using sourceVideoUrl in the dubbing API is beta-only for selected workspaces and only supports S3 signed URLs.
capabilityFacts.evidenceText: The dubbing endpoint documents both the selected-workspace gate and S3 signed URL requirement.

modelSupportItems.evidenceText: The pricing page exposes a Synthesia AI avatar library that scales from 9 to 240 plus avatars across plans.

modelSupportItems.scope: Custom persona cloning inside the platform
modelSupportItems.evidenceText: Pricing explicitly lists personal avatar counts by plan and calls unlimited personal avatars subject to fair use on Enterprise.

modelSupportItems.scope: Prompt interpretation inside the AI video assistant
modelSupportItems.evidenceText: The video creation docs explicitly state that the AI video assistant is powered by OpenAI.
The reviewed official pages disclose OpenAI usage in the assistant but do not expose a general public model-switching control or provider catalog.

uniqueValueItems.summary: Synthesia is positioned less like a raw creative model playground and more like a managed business video system for repeatable avatar, localization, and learning workflows.
uniqueValueItems.evidenceText: Pricing, SCORM, brand kit, comments, and SSO pages all push toward repeatable business operations rather than open-ended cinematic generation.

uniqueValueItems.summary: Localization is a major differentiator because Synthesia combines translation, dubbing, captions, synced updates, and a multilingual player in one managed stack.
uniqueValueItems.evidenceText: The localization page details player-level language switching, smart updates, captions, and dubbing as one coordinated workflow.

differentiationItems.positioning: enterprise video ops and avatar platform with localization workflow depth
differentiationItems.evidenceText: The official materials repeatedly emphasize workspaces, templates, comments, brand kits, SCORM, and SSO alongside avatars.

differentiationItems.positioning: Repeatable business communication and training outputs are stronger than experimental model-hopping or deep edit craft.
differentiationItems.evidenceText: The sources lean toward managed video production at scale instead of exposing a large creative model catalog.

collaborationItems.value: Users can invite collaborators to view, edit, or comment on a video, and comments live on the video overview page.
collaborationItems.evidenceText: The video overview docs explicitly list collaborators, comments, and pre-existing comment threads.

collaborationItems.value: Starter includes 1 editor and 3 guests; Creator includes 1 editor and 5 guests.
collaborationItems.evidenceText: The pricing table lists editor and guest counts per paid self-serve plan.

collaborationItems.value: Live team collaboration is marketed as an enterprise capability.
collaborationItems.evidenceText: The enterprise plan feature list includes live team collaboration.

collaborationItems.value: Admins can enforce approved brand kits across workspaces on Enterprise.
collaborationItems.evidenceText: Brand kit docs and help center both say enforcement is an enterprise admin control.

developerItems.value: API access starts on Creator or above.
developerItems.evidenceText: The API introduction explicitly states that API access is available for Creator plans or above.

developerItems.value: Creator and Enterprise API tiers have different read and write rate limits.
developerItems.evidenceText: The API introduction includes a full per-tier rate-limit table.

developerItems.value: The API can create videos from templates for batch personalized production.
developerItems.evidenceText: The create-from-template API reference and guide are dedicated to automated personalized production.

developerItems.value: Webhook creation and retrieval are part of the public API surface.
developerItems.evidenceText: The introduction page lists create webhook and retrieve webhook among the supported operations.

commercialItems.value: SCORM export is enterprise-only and downloads as a zip package for LMS use.
commercialItems.evidenceText: The SCORM help article explicitly states Enterprise-only access and outlines the export flow.

commercialItems.value: Branded video pages begin on Creator.
commercialItems.evidenceText: The Creator plan list includes branded video pages.

commercialItems.value: Published video links and embeds can be locked behind SSO for enterprise organizations.
commercialItems.evidenceText: The SSO-protected publishing article explains organization, workspace, and individual-video enforcement.

limitationItems.value: Brand kits are only available on the Enterprise plan.
limitationItems.evidenceText: The brand kits documentation explicitly states this restriction.

limitationItems.rawLabel: Dubbing on Enterprise is add-on priced
limitationItems.value: Full AI dubbing on Enterprise is a paid add-on rather than a default entitlement.
limitationItems.evidenceText: The pricing page labels Enterprise AI dubbing as a paid add-on.

limitationItems.value: API keys belong to the individual account that created them rather than the whole workspace.
limitationItems.evidenceText: The API intro warns that created API keys and webhook subscriptions are tied to the account, not the workspace.

useCaseItems.fit: Strong fit for organizations that need branded avatar videos distributed through LMS systems.
useCaseItems.evidenceText: SCORM export, comments, templates, and avatars are all explicitly documented within the official product surface.

useCaseItems.fit: A strong fit when one source video must reach audiences in many languages through one player and controlled brand surfaces.
useCaseItems.evidenceText: The localization page stresses one-click translations, multilingual player behavior, captions, and synced updates.

useCaseItems.fit: The API plus template system suit personalized videos that need repeatability more than cinematic originality.
useCaseItems.evidenceText: Synthesia's API introduction emphasizes onboarding, outbound sales, and customer lifecycle automation with templates.

userFitItems.fit: Self-serve creators can use it, but the strongest official story is still business communication rather than creator-first experimentation.
userFitItems.rationale: Self-serve plans exist, yet the core differentiators are workspaces, templates, brand pages, API, and enterprise collaboration.
userFitItems.evidenceText: Most premium features described on official pages cluster around business production and repeatability.

userFitItems.fit: Creator tier is suitable for small teams that need templated avatar output and API access with limited collaborators.
userFitItems.rationale: Creator exposes API access, multiple avatars per scene, branded video pages, and up to 5 guests.
userFitItems.evidenceText: These features are concentrated in Creator rather than Basic or Starter.

userFitItems.fit: This is where Synthesia looks strongest: governance, brand enforcement, SSO, live collaboration, SCORM, and implementation support all concentrate here.
userFitItems.rationale: Enterprise tiers unlock the controls most aligned with managed video operations at scale.
userFitItems.evidenceText: Enterprise-only features span security, publishing, localization, and onboarding support.

rawEvidenceBlocks.evidenceText: Official docs let users start from prompt, URL, document, or script and then move into a template-guided AI assistant flow.
Localization covers translation, captions, player behavior, dubbing, and smart updates in one official workflow.
The public API exposes create, update, template, asset, webhook, and dubbing endpoints with plan-specific rate limits.
Brand kits are treated as workspace-level controls with optional admin enforcement, pointing to a governance-first design for enterprise teams.
Comments, collaborator roles, analytics, sharing, and publishing are all part of the standard video lifecycle page.

unresolvedQuestions.question: Does Synthesia publish a detailed public admin matrix for RBAC beyond editors, guests, workspace admins, and organization admins?
unresolvedQuestions.reason: The reviewed sources show collaboration and admin controls, but not a comprehensive public RBAC matrix.

unresolvedQuestions.question: Which exact avatar or dubbing models sit underneath each feature tier besides the disclosed OpenAI assistant dependency?
unresolvedQuestions.reason: The official pages disclose product features and one assistant dependency, but not a broader model stack breakdown.
